capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: rate-aware surprise — the reserve a newcomer is read against tracks the recent novelty RATE, so a newcomer after a drought moves belief far more than a surface-identical newcomer mid-churn
claim: The fixed novelty reserve (NOVELTY_RESERVE=1.0) is blind to whether newcomers have been arriving: two SURFACE-IDENTICAL events — one newcomer after a long drought (E1), one newcomer in the middle of a churn (E2) — get equal surprise (in fact fixed inverts them, ranking the churn newcomer higher). Making the reserve a Born AMPLITUDE that tracks the γ-decayed newcomer rate (under the same γ as the figure field), through the UNCHANGED Born step, separates them ~4x (ctx(E1) >> ctx(E2)) and pushes the churn newcomer down into the moving-cast baseline. Identical in two senses (the core reads only atom->mass).
stimulus_shape: text+tonal · discrimination+invariance(rate) · L3 · whole-reading · target = the surpriseAt novelty reserve
verdict: CONFIRMED on the rate axis, both senses (controls dark: ctx≈fixed on steady no-newcomer lines; fixed argmaxes the loud burst, ctx halves it; omnimodal maxDiff=0). SPLIT: the pre-registered magnitude predictions (ctx argmax = E1; ctx(E1) > ctx(C_loud)) came back FALSE — a 3-at-once burst (0.576) still edges a single drought-event (0.553). Held key left as written.
mechanism: contextual-novelty-reserve: reserve(at) = γ^at·seed + Σ_{k<at} newcomers(k)·γ^(at-1-k). The γ-decayed count of newcomer arrivals before the cursor, on the same recency kernel as priorProp, handed to surpriseAt as the `novelty` amplitude. A newcomer’s KL contribution scales inversely with the reserve (traced: the drought newcomer carries 0.70 bits at reserve 0.238; the churn newcomer carries 0.18 bits at reserve 3.008). Seed 1.0 makes the opening byte-identical then decays (γ^at→0): no standing constant, absolute continuity preserved (reserve strictly positive).
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/reserve.js (pure, modality-agnostic: counts newcomers, knows nothing of atoms), exported from src/core/index.js. Wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as OPT-IN readingAt({contextualReserve:true}); the Born law (surpriseAt) is untouched — context enters only at the `novelty` amplitude.
root_cause_of_gap: the reserve was a hand-rolled constant (1.0) — an external assumption standing in for a quantity the signal should teach. A fixed reserve encodes a fixed expectation of novelty regardless of the recent rate, so steady-drift novelty and genuine-event novelty are indistinguishable. The fix is not a better constant; it is to let the recent novelty rate set the amplitude through the same fixed law.
parity: npm test 676->682 (6-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (contextualReserve opt-in, off by default; default carries no reserve field). Trace-verified the opt is non-dormant: changes bayesBits on 28/32 lines of data/esker.txt and 16/19 notes of a music doc.
generalization: the SAME interior fix produces the SAME dissociation in two senses (lexical + tonal, byte-identical) and is live through two REAL organs with no interior change (text via parseText, music via ingestMusic). Breaks no confirmed capability — exp-0001’s bridge lock stays green; the connectivity channel and default reading are untouched.
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — disjoint vocabularies give byte-identical surprise (maxDiff=0), and the rate-aware reserve flows through the music organ’s front-end unchanged. The mechanism never sees a modality; any organ emitting atoms inherits it, exactly as it inherits surpriseAt.
scope_holds: any modality whose organ deposits atoms onto the log; separating events by the recent rate of NEWCOMER arrivals (drought-event vs churn-newcomer); the contextual-amplitude principle applied to the reserve (the campaign’s named exemplar).
scope_fails: within-step COUNT normalization — the contextual reserve corrects RATE-blindness but a burst of N simultaneous newcomers still contributes N (discounted) KL terms, so a 3-at-once burst marginally outranks a single drought-event. Arguably correct (3 unknowns at once is genuinely more than 1) and NOT patched: forcing the rank would be tuning to the planted burst. A separate count-aware amplitude would be its own pressure.
